seoTitle: How to Change the Message on a QuickBooks Invoice
metaDescription: Customize the message field on your QuickBooks invoices to improve client communication, from thank-you notes to payment reminders.
intro: The message on an invoice is often the last thing a client reads before paying, so getting it right can improve both clarity and how professional your business appears. This guide walks through updating invoice messages in QuickBooks, including tips for recurring invoices.

steps.heading: Access invoice message settings
steps.body: Go to the invoice customization area in QuickBooks where message fields can be edited.

steps.heading: Update the message text
steps.body: Replace the default or existing message with wording that fits the invoice's purpose, such as a thank-you note or payment reminder.

steps.heading: Apply the message to a specific invoice or as a default
steps.body: Decide whether the new message should apply to just the current invoice or become the default for future invoices.

steps.heading: Review before sending
steps.body: Preview the invoice to make sure the message reads clearly and fits the tone you want for client communication.

faq.question: Can I set a different message for recurring invoices?
faq.answer: Yes, you can customize the message on recurring invoices separately from one-off invoices to fit ongoing billing needs.

faq.question: Will changing the invoice message affect invoices already sent?
faq.answer: No, message updates only apply to new or unsent invoices, not ones already delivered to clients.

faq.question: What should I include in an invoice message?
faq.answer: A good invoice message typically covers a thank-you note, payment terms, or specific details about the service, keeping it clear and professional.

faq.question: Can I automate invoice messages for different types of billing?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks allows you to set default messages that automatically apply, which can save time on recurring or templated invoices.
